title: Strong Mpemba Effect Through a Reentrant Phase Transition
authors: Kristian Blom, Doron Benyamin, Uwe Thiele, Oren Raz, Aljaz Godec
abstract: We investigate temperature quenches across the reentrant phase transition of the antiferromagnetic Ising model in a magnetic field and show that the strong direct and inverse Mpemba effects arise when quenches terminate in the paramagnetic phase. These anomalous relaxation phenomena originate from the selective excitation of the slowest relaxation mode, which in the paramagnetic phase is purely staggered. Consequently, quenches starting from the paramagnetic phase have zero overlap with the slow mode and exhibit a strong (inverse) Mpemba effect. Quenches from the antiferromagnetic phase excite the staggered mode and display a slow-relaxation tail. By varying the lattice coordination number we show that the strong Mpemba effect disappears in the absence of reentrance. Our results provide the first demonstration of the strong (inverse) Mpemba effect in the antiferromagnetic Ising model based on the pair-approximation, and establish a link between anomalous relaxation and the equilibrium phase behavior.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the strong Mpemba effect in an antiferromagnetic Ising model is driven by reentrant phase transitions. It shows that the effect occurs because quenches into the paramagnetic phase avoid exciting the slowest staggered relaxation mode. The work establishes a direct connection between equilibrium phase behavior and anomalous non-equilibrium relaxation.
main_problem: Investigating the origin of the strong direct and inverse Mpemba effects during temperature quenches and establishing a link between these anomalous relaxation phenomena and reentrant phase transitions.
main_result: The strong Mpemba effect arises when temperature quenches terminate in the paramagnetic phase due to the selective excitation of the slowest relaxation mode, a phenomenon that disappears in the absence of reentrance.
method: The authors use the Bethe-Guggenheim (pair) approximation to derive macroscopic dynamical equations and analyze the spectral decomposition of the Jacobian matrix to study relaxation modes.
model_or_system: An antiferromagnetic Ising model in a uniform magnetic field on a Bethe lattice with varying coordination number z, governed by single spin-flip Glauber dynamics.
key_observables: Sublattice magnetizations (ma, mb), total magnetization (m), staggered magnetization (s), nearest-neighbor correlation (q), and excess free energy (D(t)).
important_parameters: Temperature (T), magnetic field (H), coordination number (z), and exchange interaction (J).
main_assumptions: The results are based on the Bethe-Guggenheim pair approximation and assume the thermodynamic limit (N to infinity).
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows equilibrium order parameters and phase diagrams for different coordination numbers, demonstrating monotonic vs. reentrant phase boundaries. Figure 2 illustrates the decay of excess free energy and the crossing of relaxation curves during quenches.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of anomalous relaxation, defines the Ising model and BG approximation, derives the dynamical equations and free energy, analyzes the reentrant phase transition mechanism, and concludes with the spectral analysis of relaxation modes and the emergence of the Mpemba effect.
why_interesting: The study provides deep insights into how equilibrium phase topology (reentrance) can fundamentally alter non-equilibrium relaxation dynamics and the selection of slow modes in many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Emergence of reentrant phase behavior with increasing coordination number. (a)–(c) Equilibrium values [Eq. (11)] of the staggered magnetization s, total magnetization m, and nearest-neighbor correlation q as functions of temperature T at fixed magnetic field H/z|J|=0.5 for z=3<z∗≈5.14. Solid lines denote stable equilibria, dashed lines unstable ones. (d) Corresponding phase diagram in the (H, T) plane. The critical line (green) is monotonic and no reentrant phase transition is observed. (e)–(g) Equilibrium values of (s, m, q) for z=7>z∗and H/z|J|=1.02. In contrast to z=3, the staggered magnetization is nonzero only within an intermediate temperature window. (h) Corresponding phase...

FIG. 2. Mpemba and inverse Mpemba effects across the reentrant regime and their spectral origin. (a) Displace- ment from equilibrium D(t) [Eq. (13)] for a cooling quench from Ti1 = 3 (paramagnetic phase) and Ti2 = 2 (antiferromagnetic phase) to Tf = 0.12 (paramagnetic phase). Although the quench from Ti2 starts closer to equilibrium, the relaxation curves cross at finite time, showing the Mpemba effect. Insets show the corresponding relaxation trajectories in the (m, s) plane, zoomed in around the trajectories; blue and red dots denote the initial states, and the black dot the final equilibrium.
